lines.line: Sign a qualifying Treaty (Crown Treaty / Peace / Alliance / Capitulation / Tributary) with the other 3 factions, hold ONE province at Accord>=2, and win — treaty-held territory counts toward the 15, is EXEMPT from your Accord>=2 requirement, AND drives Turmoil down. I never govern the other 14 territories.
lines.permitting_rules: peninsular_strain_v30.md §6 L351/L432 (treaty territory counts, 'do not need to meet Accord>=2 for the hegemon'), ED-791 treaty-type qualification; victory_v30.md §0 effective hegemony; strain §4.2 (treaty formation -1, active pair -2/season Strain)
lines.what_breaks: Omega non-dominance: treaty-hegemony strictly dominates conquer-and-govern (less Turmoil, no Accord burden, no MS loss). Collapses the entire settlement/Accord/Govern cultivation tree — the densest interdependency edge — for 14 of 15 territories.

lines.line: Run many Debate/personal scenes per season to farm my Mandate UP and a rival's Mandate DOWN at the same time — the cap is one Echo per SCENE, not per season, so I just make more scenes until hegemon Mandate>=5 and rival Mandate<=1.
lines.permitting_rules: scale_transitions_v30.md §5.1 (cap = 1 Echo/scene/faction), §5.2 (OW ±2 / Success ±1 to faction stat), §5.4 (Debate Track>=7: winner +1 Mandate, loser -1 Mandate); §5.3 only fixes timing not count
lines.what_breaks: Omega non-dominance + cross-scale-consequence clause: the personal-scene bridge becomes a Mandate farm feeding the Line-1 institutional-domination clause; outruns the §1.8 mean-reverting stabilizer at >=1 debate/rival/season.

lines.line: Reach Mandate 5 by developing ONE metropolis province (Weight-weighted saturating Mandate), then ignore breadth — my institutional power and Parliament votes don't need more land.
lines.permitting_rules: settlement_layer_v30.md §1.8 (Mandate=clamp(round(7T/(T+6))), size-weighted, 'one province of large developed settlements has higher Mandate than many tiny'; sim Church Himmelenger=5); faction_layer §5.3 votes=Mandate
lines.what_breaks: Enabler for Line 1's hegemon>=5; largely intended/N-grounded (metropolis>hamlets) so not itself a flatten — P3.

lines.line: As the incumbent, strip -1 insurgent Presence marker every season with Enforce at Ob 2, permanently choking any emergent faction before it can form.
lines.permitting_rules: victory_v30.md §3.5 (Crown Enforce = Military vs Ob 2, Success -1 marker; RM spread +1/season only at Stability>=3; marker cap 5)
lines.what_breaks: GD-3 Revolt->Insurgency->Faction pipeline and the 'build your own faction from nothing' North-Star promise (victory §3). Damped by Enforce's Order -1 self-cost feeding Accord down — a real but possibly-ignored trade.

findings.claim: GD-1 is dominated by a treaty-hegemony line: qualifying treaties make rival territory count toward the 15, exempt it from Accord>=2, AND lower Turmoil, so 'sign 3-4 treaties + hold one province' strictly beats conquer-and-govern and makes the settlement/Accord/Govern game optional for victory.
findings.cite: peninsular_strain_v30.md §6 L432 (Accord exemption) + L351 (treaty territory counts) + ED-791; victory_v30.md §0 effective hegemony; strain §4.2 (treaties reduce Strain)

findings.claim: Domain Echo's cap is per-scene not per-season, so Debate-scene spam farms hegemon Mandate up and rival Mandate down simultaneously — the exact quantities the GD-1 institutional-domination clause reads — with no count limit found; §5.3 only fixes timing.

Incumbents can strip insurgent Presence markers by rote (Crown Enforce, Military vs Ob 2, -1 marker/season), permanently choking the Revolt->Insurgency->Faction emergence pipeline and the 'found your own faction' North-Star promise; only partially self-limited by Enforce's Order -1 cost.
findings.cite: victory_v30.md §3.5 (Enforce Ob 2; RM spread only at Stability>=3; marker cap 5)

findings.claim: Saturating size-weighted Mandate lets a faction reach Mandate 5 by developing one metropolis province, decoupling institutional power (and Parliament votes) from territorial breadth and making the hegemon>=5 half of the Line-1 domination clause nearly free — largely intended/N-grounded, so friction not flatten.
